timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A police file reveals Walter's past conviction; Sergeant Lucas, mid-fifties, watches him being fingerprinted, reflecting on his criminal history.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Lucas reviews Walter's criminal record and observes him during booking, establishing a stance of suspicion and institutional control.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: assessing Walter as a known criminal risk
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Lucas begins monitoring Walter based on past conviction
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: potential for bias or overreach in targeting Walter
evidence_quotes: A mug shot reveals Walter, many years younger.
Words stand out -- "Convicted, 1st degree --," "3 counts --," "served --."
A plain clothes officer closes the folder and looks out his office window where Walter stands being fingerprinted.

relation_updates: views Walter as a suspect with violent history

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sergeant Lucas aggressively interrogates Walter, threatening and demeaning him, while Walter remains silent and distressed; after Lucas leaves, Walter experiences a disturbing vision of a girl with a red ball.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Lucas enters Walter's apartment without consent and conducts an aggressive, unprofessional interrogation, using threats and degrading language to imply Walter's guilt in recent attacks on girls.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: intimidate Walter into compliance or confession
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Lucas leaves after asserting dominance through verbal abuse and implied violence, establishing a coercive presence in Walter's private space.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: abuse of power masked as law enforcement diligence
evidence_quotes: Sergeant Lucas pushes the door open further and walks right in. He wears a well-tailored suit.
We know every step you make, every goddamn step! We know when you sleep, when you eat, when you shit, and when you jack off.
Think anyone would miss you if I threw you out the window right now? I could say you jumped when I came in. Who are they going to believe?

relation_updates: treats Walter as guilty without evidence
status_updates: exerts unchecked authority in private space

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter, overwhelmed, tears his journal apart, shouting defiance; a vision of a girl appears, but he shuts it out by closing his eyes and covering his ears, stopping the sound.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: applying psychological pressure during an interrogation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Lucas visits Walter unannounced and delivers a disturbing account of a child's murder to unsettle him, dismissing any notion of moral redemption or innocence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: undermine Walter’s emotional stability through graphic storytelling
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Lucas presents himself as a hardened figure who rejects hope or moral comfort in the face of evil
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the visit blurs whether Lucas is conducting an investigation or pursuing a personal, punitive agenda
evidence_quotes: Ten days later they find Adele's body. Or what's left of it.
Ain't no fucking woodsman in this world.

relation_updates: frames himself as the only truth-teller in a brutal world
auxiliary.persona_anchor: enforcer who uses traumatic narratives to assert control

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter, overwhelmed, tears his journal apart, shouting defiance; a vision of a girl appears, but he shuts it out by closing his eyes and covering his ears, stopping the sound.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Lucas verbally accosts Walter, calling him 'scum,' and slaps him on the back of the head before leaving.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: assert dominance and express contempt toward Walter
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Walter becomes agitated and retreats into isolation, tearing up his journal and shutting out a hallucinatory vision
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ongoing tension between Lucas and Walter, with potential for future conflict
evidence_quotes: I don't know why they keep letting scum like you return to the streets.
He slaps Walter on the back of the head...
... and exits.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: physically assertive enforcer crossing professional lines

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Lucas questions Walter about an assault; the victim, wanted for raping a boy, can't speak due to injuries. Lucas warns he's watching Walter, who remains calm but unsettled.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Lucas questions Walter about a recent assault across the street, notes a scratch on his neck, reveals the victim was raped a boy and cannot speak, and warns he is under surveillance.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: gather information and pressure Walter into revealing involvement
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Walter maintains composure but exhibits subtle discomfort, such as fixating on rolling and unrolling socks, and discarding them when he realizes the action
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Walter was involved in the assault and whether Lucas has enough evidence to act
evidence_quotes: A man was badly beaten across the street. You know anything about that?
That's a nasty scratch on your neck.
Unfortunately, the victim can't talk. In addition to his other injuries, his jaw is broken.
Stay out of trouble, Walter. Cause I'll be watching you.

relation_updates: intensifies psychological pressure in their relationship
status_updates: asserts investigative control through pointed questioning

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Sergeant Lucas emerges as a persistent and antagonistic authority figure who systematically targets Walter, leveraging his position to interrogate, intimidate, and psychologically destabilize him. Over successive encounters, Lucas shifts from procedural oversight to overt coercion, using verbal abuse, physical gestures, and trauma-laden narratives to assert control. His actions suggest a blurred line between investigation and personal vendetta, contributing to an escalating tension that defines his adversarial relationship with Walter. The timeline reflects Lucas’s role as a catalyst of pressure rather than a neutral investigator.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The 5 o'clock whistle blows; workers leave. Walter punches out silently after being jostled, exits. Vicki watches from a distance in line.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki silently observes Walter being jostled at the punch-out line, marking her first noticed attention toward him.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Vicki registers Walter's quiet endurance of workplace roughness.
evidence_quotes: Walter stands in line, keeping to himself.
Vicki, a tough-looking but striking woman, stands in line a little further back watching.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Vicki leaves in her Jeep after fending off catcallers; Walter waits at a bus stop, observing her departure and seeking shelter from the cold, as a provocative ad fills the kiosk nearby.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki responds to catcalling coworkers by flipping them off and driving away in her Jeep, showing resistance to harassment.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Vicki physically distances herself from the harassment and exits the scene on her own terms.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ongoing gender-based workplace tension
evidence_quotes: a car pulls up next to her and men catcall and whistle out the window.
Vicki flicks them off.
She gets in her Jeep and leaves, tearing by the bus stop.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Vicki, after confronting Pedro and being laughed at, storms off angrily, kicking wood at a man and brushing past Walter, who watches her leave.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki confronts Pedro for inappropriate behavior, strikes him with her gloves, and leaves the scene after being mocked by coworkers.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Vicki is visibly angry and isolated as she exits the warehouse, having failed to gain support for her protest.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: lack of support for her stance on workplace conduct
evidence_quotes: Walter looks across the way to see Vicki yelling furiously and smacking a GUY around the head with her work gloves.
Vicki looks around and storms off, kicking a piece of wood into one of the men, hard.
She brushes past Walter on her way to the exit.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter accepts a ride from Vicki in her Jeep as Sergeant Lucas arrives and observes them before entering the office.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki offers Walter a ride, breaking prior avoidance by initiating direct contact.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: connect with Walter
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Walter gets into Vicki's Jeep, beginning a shared ride despite initial hesitation.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Walter's hesitation and initial refusal indicate lingering discomfort or caution.
evidence_quotes: Vicki's rusty old Jeep pulls up alongside him and stops, honking to grab his attention.
VICKI Want a ride?
WALTER I'm all right.
VICKI (cont'd) Come on, I won't bite.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Vicki and Walter exchange banter about their quiet, isolated natures; he admits he avoided her, assuming she was a lesbian, sparking playful tension as she challenges his assumption.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki responds to Walter's assumption that she is a lesbian with a flirtatious challenge, prompting mutual personal disclosure.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: test connection with Walter
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: shifts their interaction from neutral coworkers to one with romantic undertones.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Walter's assumption reveals a misjudgment that Vicki must navigate in their developing relationship.
evidence_quotes: WALTER I thought you were a dyke.
VICKI What do you think?
She shoots him a look.
Vicki takes out a cigarette and presses the cigarette lighter in, revealing a tattoo on her wrist of a pair of breasts with angel wings.

relation_updates: responds to Walter's 'dyke' comment with flirtatious challenge

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Vicki wants to stay the night after sex; Walter resists with excuses about insomnia and nightmares, but she remains unfazed and settles in despite his discomfort.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki insists on staying the night despite Walter's objections about insomnia and nightmares.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain closeness and challenge Walter's isolation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Vicki remains in bed and settles in for the night, despite Walter's discomfort and attempts to dissuade her.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Walter's insomnia and nightmares are acknowledged but not resolved, leaving his emotional and physical distress unaddressed.
evidence_quotes: VICKI I thought I'd stay the night.
WALTER I suffer from insomnia.
WALTER When I do sleep, I sweat a lot. Usually I get nightmares and wake up screaming.
VICKI (smiling) I sleep like a dead horse. Anything else?

relation_updates: chooses to stay overnight, defying his discomfort

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter tells Vicki to leave; she dresses, looks at him as he sits motionless, and departs.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki hears Walter confess to being a convicted child molester after sharing a personal betrayal, disrupting her emotional openness with shock and discomfort.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seek emotional connection
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: visibly shaken, emotionally withdrawn after Walter's confession
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to process attraction alongside moral repulsion
evidence_quotes: VICKI Fucked my best friend's husband.
WALTER I molested little girls.
Vicki is visibly shaken. She puts the cigarette out.
Why don't you just go now, okay?

relation_updates: emotional bond ruptured by Walter's confession
auxiliary.persona_anchor: self-aware but destabilized by conflicting emotions

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter confesses his self-inflicted past destroyed his family and dreams; Vicki challenges him to change her mind, leading to a tense emotional confrontation and a tentative embrace.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki confronts Walter about his past actions and demands he justify his change, prompting Walter to reveal how his own choices destroyed his family, leading to a moment of emotional vulnerability and a tentative embrace.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Walter seeks to be seen as changed; Vicki tests whether his remorse is genuine.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Vicki responds to Walter's confession with a physical gesture of connection, indicating a shift from skepticism to cautious emotional openness.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Walter's past behavior, especially his justification for targeting young girls, remains a source of tension and doubt.
evidence_quotes: VICKI I want you to change my mind!
I lost a wife and a daughter. It hurt to lose my wife. It killed me to lose my daughter.
After a long pause, she approaches and tentatively pulls him into an embrace.

relation_updates: shift from distant to emotionally vulnerable

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Vicki reveals she was abused by all three of her brothers, whom she claims to love despite their actions, and states they would deny it violently if confronted.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki reveals she was sexually abused by all three of her brothers, whom she says she still loves, and explains they would violently deny any accusation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Vicki shares her trauma on her own terms while maintaining emotional control.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Walter learns about Vicki's history of abuse and her conflicted loyalty to her brothers.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The abuse has never been confronted, and her brothers would respond with violence if accused.
evidence_quotes: One day I'll tell you how I survived as the youngest in a family of three sons.
I got poked around... here and there.
All three -- in chronological order.
Not ever.

relation_updates: reveals emotional complexity in her family ties

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter struggles with self-doubt, rejects living together, and impulsively ends the relationship; Vicki leaves immediately, leaving him surprised and conflicted.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki immediately leaves after Walter ends their relationship and rejects her proposal to live together.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Vicki abandons the goal of deepening the relationship following Walter's rejection of cohabitation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Vicki exits the relationship without further discussion, indicating emotional disengagement.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Walter's emotional unavailability remains unaddressed.
evidence_quotes: VICKI Move in with me.
Vicki puts her fork down, pulls on her jacket and exits.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Vicki confronts Mary-Kay about Walter, searches her desk, finds notification fliers, threatens violence if someone returns, and storms out after throwing papers at Bob.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki confronts Mary-Kay about Walter, searches her desk, finds notification fliers, and issues a direct threat of violence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: find Walter and stop the notification
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Vicki has escalated to physical aggression and overt threats, positioning herself in open conflict with Mary-Kay and Bob.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Walter's safety and the distribution of the notification fliers
evidence_quotes: Vicki picks up Mary-Kay's mug and pours coffee on her lap.
Vicki tears through Mary-Kay's drawers throwing contents to the floor.
VICKI If she's here tomorrow, I'll fucking kill her.
Vicki throws the papers at Bob and storms out.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Vicki and Walter drive at sunset; she notices a flock of starlings, which captivates them both momentarily, easing tension as she holds his hand.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vicki reaches for Walter's hand and directs his attention to a flock of starlings, creating a brief moment of shared focus and emotional ease during a tense drive.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking connection despite prior resistance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: temporary emotional softening between Vicki and Walter
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ongoing tension beneath the surface, unaddressed by conversation
evidence_quotes: Vicki reaches for his hand
then something catches her eye.
VICKI (cont'd) Starlings.
Vicki looks at him and smiles. There is a long beat as they drive.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Vicki evolves from a guarded, isolated worker into a woman who actively pursues connection with Walter, challenging social norms and her own defenses. Her relationship with him becomes a crucible for confronting trauma, moral ambiguity, and emotional risk. After cycles of intimacy, rupture, and reconciliation, she shifts from defiance to deep involvement, ultimately becoming a witness to Walter’s unresolved past and a participant in their shared emotional reckoning.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter, a man in his early forties with a hardened look, feeds a log into a wood chipper and wipes sweat and grime from his face.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walter operates a wood chipper at a lumberyard, handling logs and showing signs of physical exertion.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: established as a laborer performing demanding physical work in an industrial setting
evidence_quotes: A man finishes feeding the log into the chipper.
He pauses to wipe the sweat and grime from his face.
He is WALTER, early forties, features handsome but hardened by time.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Walter as a man defined by physical toil and emotional reserve

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter is being fingerprinted by a female officer at a counter.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walter is fingerprinted by a police officer, indicating he is undergoing official documentation within the justice system.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: formally recorded by authorities through fingerprinting
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: reason for fingerprinting and current legal status
evidence_quotes: Walter stands at a counter
a female officer fingerprints each finger

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A police file reveals Walter's past conviction; Sergeant Lucas, mid-fifties, watches him being fingerprinted, reflecting on his criminal history.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: A police file discloses Walter's first-degree conviction and prior incarceration, confirmed through a mug shot and official documents.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Walter is identified as a previously convicted individual now undergoing police processing.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: impact of past conviction on current situation
evidence_quotes: CLOSE on a police file. A mug shot reveals Walter, many years younger.
Words stand out -- "Convicted, 1st degree --," "3 counts --," "served --."
where Walter stands being fingerprinted.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter refuses therapist Rosen's suggestion to keep a journal, dismissing it as a bad idea and rejecting the concept of reflection despite Rosen's explanation of its benefits.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walter rejects Rosen's suggestion to keep a journal, dismissing reflection as unnecessary and mocking the therapeutic rationale behind it.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: avoids emotional vulnerability
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Walter remains closed off and dismissive of introspective practices
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ongoing resistance to self-reflection and therapeutic processes
evidence_quotes: WALTER A diary?
WALTER No way.
WALTER Diaries have sent too many guys to prison.
WALTER Bad idea.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Walter as emotionally guarded and skeptical of therapy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Vicki and Walter's intimate encounter unfolds in fragmented scenes, progressing from undressing to passionate sex, then ending with Walter alone, washing his face, reflecting in silence.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walter engages in physical intimacy with Vicki while declining to discuss his past, maintaining emotional distance despite escalating closeness.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: avoid emotional exposure while accepting physical connection
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally guarded but physically engaged
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: refuses to share his past when Vicki asks directly
evidence_quotes: WALTER No.
She kisses him. Hesitantly, he kisses her back.
Walter lies back on his bed. Vicki runs her hand up his chest, pulling his shirt over his head.
Walter looks at Vicki, already topless. He tentatively touches her breast.

status_updates: experiences intimate contact after prolonged isolation
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Walter as capable of physical but not emotional intimacy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter sits by a window, observing birds and children while hesitating with a notebook before beginning to write about kids arriving at school.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walter begins writing in his journal after hesitating, prompted by observing children outside.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to document observations of children arriving at school
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: begins writing his observations of children arriving at school
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: hesitation about whether to continue writing
evidence_quotes: Walter sits next to the window watching birds play at the bird feeder.
There is the SOUND of children playing outside.
Walter opens the notebook, closes it, then opens it again.
He looks up and sees his reflection in the window.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter tells Vicki to leave; she dresses, looks at him as he sits motionless, and departs.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walter reveals he was imprisoned for molesting young girls, responds to Vicki's attempt to stay by telling her to leave, and reasserts emotional distance after a moment of physical intimacy.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to end the encounter and push Vicki away after disclosing his past
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Walter is emotionally isolated again, having rejected Vicki's presence following his confession
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: struggle with shame and inability to sustain connection despite desire for closeness
evidence_quotes: WALTER I molested little girls.
Walter gets out of bed and pulls on his pants.
WALTER Get your kicks somewhere else.

relation_updates: disclosure of traumatic past destroys intimacy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: On a bright Saturday, a bus arrives at a mall where passengers, including Walter, exit. Walter narrates that he followed a girl.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walter begins following a girl, marking a shift from passive observation to impulsive action.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: following a girl
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Walter is acting on an unexplained impulse to follow someone.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: why Walter chose to follow the girl
evidence_quotes: Walter is among them.
WALTER (V.O.) I followed a girl.

relation_updates: focus shifts to an unknown girl
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Walter as someone acting on uncontrolled urges

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter, overwhelmed, tears his journal apart, shouting defiance; a vision of a girl appears, but he shuts it out by closing his eyes and covering his ears, stopping the sound.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walter destroys his journal after being confronted by Lucas and then blocks out a vision and sound of a girl by closing his eyes and covering his ears.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: rejecting external judgment and internal guilt
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Walter shuts down emotionally, actively cutting off both external confrontation and internal reminders.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the origin and meaning of the girl vision and Walter's guilt remain unaddressed
evidence_quotes: He slaps Walter on the back of the head...
Walter rushes to the blinds, drawing them down, then scrambles to get his journal from the drawer.
He drops to his knees, tearing the pages from his journal, tearing them to bits.
WALTER You have no right to talk to... to speak like... I am not!... I am not!

relation_updates: relationship with Lucas becomes overtly hostile
emotional connection to the girl vision is revealed but suppressed
status_updates: emotional state deteriorates to breaking point
journal destroyed, symbolizing rejection of self-reflection

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter violently attacks Candy, experiencing flashbacks, then stops, looks at his bloodied hands, and flees as Candy lies injured on the ground.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walter attacks Candy in a state of flashback, then stops and flees after seeing his bloodied hands, showing a return to awareness.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: confront past trauma through violent action
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: traumatized and fugitive
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: unprocessed childhood abuse
evidence_quotes: Walter no longer sees Candy but himself -- many years younger.
Walter watches, fear and emotion coursing through his face.
He pounds and pounds on Candy, then finally pulls away.
He looks at the blood on his hands, which shake uncontrollably.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Walter as both perpetrator and victim of violence

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter stands in the shower, turns on the water, and begins to sob.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walter begins to sob while standing in the shower, showing visible emotional distress following a traumatic event.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally overwhelmed and grieving
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: emotional aftermath of violence involving Candy
evidence_quotes: Walter stands in the shower.
He turns the water on
lets it run over him
Slowly, he begins to sob.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walter expresses guilt and uncertainty about being forgiven; Rosen emphasizes it will take time, and Walter reflects on this, accepting the need for patience in his emotional healing process.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walter acknowledges the difficulty of being forgiven by someone he hurt and expresses hope for acceptance, while accepting that healing requires time.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: achieve self-forgiveness and be accepted by those he hurt
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Walter moves from uncertainty to a quiet acknowledgment of the need for patience in his healing process.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether time will lead to actual reconciliation with the person he hurt
evidence_quotes: She's still really hurt... and angry. I don't know... if she will ever... forgive me.
I understand that. I do. I just hope... I just want her to...
Time.
I feel... okay.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Walter as someone beginning to accept the possibility of change

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Walter, a man with a criminal past involving child molestation, reintegrates into society under strict legal and emotional constraints. He navigates isolation, surveillance, and strained relationships while grappling with trauma, desire, and the possibility of change. His journey moves from emotional withdrawal to tentative connection, self-reflection, and ultimately a violent confrontation with a predator that mirrors his own past, culminating in psychological reckoning and a fragile openness to healing.
